_note: ADR-0147 — ui-code-editor richtext live-preview mode. Extends the ALREADY-SHIPPED ./editor surface (ADR-0139) rather than rebuilding it — nodes touch ONLY code/src/editor/* (editor.ts/cm-editor.ts/editor.css/editor.md + a NEW cm-richtext.ts) and the editor's own gates. OUTSIDE-IN nodes = the surfaces the mode touches; INSIDE-OUT actions = what the extended editor must newly DO, crossed via hosts. plan:true — every leaf carries a checkable accept predicate. Kim pre-ratified the two architecture forks (CM6 decorations, mode lives on ui-code-editor) — no node re-opens them. entry-list.ts's five call sites are deliberately NOT nodes: default mode='source' keeps them byte-unchanged (the ADR's compatibility law); ui-markdown / ./highlight / a2ui are untouched by construction.

nodes.label: ui-code-editor — richtext live-preview mode (one component, markdown the only model)
the `mode` prop (source|richtext, default source, reflected) + the inert-fallback law (editor.ts)
nodes.accept: `npm test editor` — jsdom: mode defaults to 'source' and reflects; setting mode='richtext' under jsdom (no CM mount possible) leaves the plain surface, value, FACE participation, and input/change timing byte-identical to today (the inert-fallback law, ADR-0147 cl.6); an unknown mode value behaves as 'source'

nodes.label: the CmHandle richtext seam — `setRichtext(on)` via a new richtextCompartment + `richtextAvailable` on the handle (cm-editor.ts)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser editor` — both engines: after mount, handle.setRichtext(true/false) reconfigures the live view with NO document change, NO selection loss, NO history reset (undo still reaches pre-toggle edits); richtextAvailable is true iff the lang-markdown pack loaded (false ⇒ the toggle never renders)

nodes.label: cm-richtext.ts — the decoration engine (a pure VIEW transform over the same EditorView document)

construct decorations — ATX headings, strong, emphasis, inline code, links, unordered-list bullets, blockquote: style the construct, hide its markup (Decoration.mark/line/replace + a bullet WidgetType over the lang-markdown syntax tree)
nodes.accept:
`npm run test:browser editor` — both engines: with mode='richtext' and value '## Title
**bold** _em_ `code`
- item
> quote
[go](https://x)', the visible DOM (cursor parked on a blank line) contains NONE of the raw marks (##, **, _, backticks, -, >, [, ](https://x)) while the construct text is present, the heading line carries rt-h2 with a larger computed font-size, the strong span computes font-weight ≥ 600, and a bullet widget replaces the list mark; a fenced code block's fences + contents stay verbatim (deliberately undecorated)

nodes.label: reveal-near-cursor — the selection's line(s) suppress the HIDE decorations (styling stays), the standard live-preview UX
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser editor` — both engines: placing the selection on the '## Title' line makes '##' visible in the DOM (the line KEEPS its rt-h2 class/size); moving the selection to another line hides it again; a multi-line range selection reveals every intersected line's marks

nodes.label: link activation — modifier-click (Cmd/Ctrl) opens the link's URL; plain click stays cursor placement (editing remains primary)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser editor` — both engines: Cmd/Ctrl+click on a decorated link invokes window.open (stubbed) with the URL resolved from the syntax tree, 'noopener'; a plain click does NOT open and instead places the cursor in the link text

nodes.label: the mode-toggle part (editor.ts) — availability-gated, keyboard-operable, aria-pressed, emits `toggle` on user action
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser editor` — both engines: after CM enhancement with richtextAvailable, a [data-part='mode-toggle'] (role=button, tabindex 0, aria-pressed mirroring mode==='richtext') renders; click AND Enter AND Space each flip `mode` and emit ONE host-targeted `toggle`; a programmatic mode set emits NOTHING and still syncs aria-pressed; disabled hosts render no operable toggle, readonly hosts keep it operable; jsdom leg: no CM ⇒ the part never exists

nodes.label: editor.css — the rt-* class block + --ui-code-editor-rt-* tokens, toggle-part styling (sticky corner), forced-colors degrade, and the :not([hidden]) visibility guard on every new part rule
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser editor` — both engines: rt-h1..h6 sizes ride --ui-code-editor-rt-* tokens fed from the editor's own font token; forced-colors leg keeps every rt-* construct legible as CanvasText (weight/style survive — SPEC-C5: no information rides hue alone); EVERY new display rule targeting a [data-part] carries :not([hidden]) (the guard whose absence on [data-part='editor'] already caused the duplicate-surface bug — asserted by a css-text probe or the browser visibility leg); no transition animates the mode switch (it SNAPS; reduced-motion owes nothing new)

nodes.label: confinement.test.ts — the DESIGNATED allowlist widens to the lazy PAIR {cm-editor.ts, cm-richtext.ts}; every other invariant byte-identical
nodes.accept: `npm test confinement` — the gate proves: cm-richtext.ts statically imports CM (anti-vacuous), cm-richtext.ts is statically imported ONLY by cm-editor.ts (stays inside the ONE lazy chunk), editor.ts still carries ZERO static CM imports and still reaches cm-editor.ts via dynamic import only, and NO static CM import exists anywhere else under code/src

nodes.label: editor.md descriptor — the mode attribute row, the mode-toggle part, the toggle event, keyboard rows; the descriptor↔props trip-wire stays green
nodes.accept: `npm test editor-descriptor` — the frontmatter gains mode (attributes), mode-toggle (parts), toggle (events), and the toggle's keyboard rows; compareDescriptorToProps passes against the extended static props; the prose section documents the inert-fallback + availability law

nodes.label: cross-mode identity legs — value/FACE/event-timing byte-identical in both modes; round-trip is vacuous by construction and PROVEN anyway
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser editor` — both engines: type into a richtext-mode editor ⇒ .value is the exact markdown string (marks included); flipping mode source↔richtext (repeatedly) leaves .value, the form value, the undo history, and the committed/change baseline untouched; blur-with-change timing fires identically in both modes; jsdom leg: the FACE suite (reset/restore/validity) passes with mode='richtext' set

nodes.label: size + identity gates — decoration bytes land in the LAZY chunk only; default barrels stay CM-free and byte-identical
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green (identity, barrels, layering untouched); `npm run size` re-measures the lazy CM chunk line-item (cm-richtext.ts joins it) — the figure moves, the main-graph budgets do NOT; zero new npm dependencies in code/package.json (the gen-ui-kit undeclared-CM-package P0 cannot recur because nothing new is imported)

nodes.label: independent component-reviewer GO before commit
nodes.accept: a fresh-context component-reviewer pass returns GO for the extended ui-code-editor against ADR-0147's clause sheet (mode contract, decoration/reveal correctness, toggle a11y, inert-fallback, confinement/size gates) — generator ≠ critic

actions.label: declare a mode (source|richtext) on the host — default source, reflected, inert wherever CM cannot mount
reconfigure the LIVE CodeMirror view's richtext layer at runtime via a Compartment, preserving doc/selection/history
decorate the v1 markdown constructs — style the construct, hide its markup — as a pure view transform over the same document
reveal raw markup on the line(s) the selection occupies, keeping the styling
open a link's URL on modifier-click while plain click stays cursor placement
offer a built-in, keyboard-operable, state-announcing mode toggle that appears only when richtext is actually available
keep .value, FACE participation, and input/change timing byte-identical in both modes — markdown stays the ONLY model
paint the richtext constructs from the component's own token chain, degrade under forced-colors, and guard every new part's visibility CSS
keep the CM confinement invariant — editor.ts static-CM-free, all decoration code inside the one lazy chunk
keep the public descriptor in lockstep with the new surface
keep the size/identity gates green with decoration bytes confined to the lazy chunk
gate the build on an independent reviewer GO

edges.why: the compartment (n3) wraps the decoration extension n5 builds — the extension must exist to be compartment-configured
the host mode effect (n2's build completion) drives handle.setRichtext, which n3 defines
reveal-near-cursor (n6) is a suppression pass inside the SAME decoration builder n5 writes
link activation (n7) resolves the URL from the same syntax-tree walk and targets the spans n5 decorates
the toggle (n8) flips the mode prop n2 declares
the toggle's availability gate reads richtextAvailable, which n3 exposes
the rt-* classes n9 styles are emitted by n5's decorations
the toggle part n9 styles (sticky corner, guard, forced-colors) is created by n8
the confinement allowlist widening (n10) names the new module n5 creates
the descriptor (n11) documents the prop n2 adds
the descriptor (n11) documents the part/event/keyboard surface n8 adds
the cross-mode identity legs (n12) flip the prop n2 declares
the history/selection-preservation identity claims (n12) ride the compartment reconfigure n3 builds
review (n14) grades reveal correctness, which must be built first
review (n14) grades the CSS guard + forced-colors legs, which must be built first
review (n14) grades the identity claims, which must be proven first
review (n14) runs against a green gate set, never a red one
